mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: High inflation (CPIAUCSL > 240) signals central bank tightening, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as investors price in higher discount rates.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 240.

mechanism_narrative: Strong industrial production (INDPRO) and employment growth (PAYEMS) indicate robust economic activity, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days through improved corporate earnings prospects.
failure_condition: SPX returns are negative when both INDPRO and PAYEMS show positive momentum over 20-day horizons.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, rising FEDFUNDS pressures equity valuations through higher discount rates, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns during periods when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Low unemployment (UNRATE) and strong employment growth (PAYEMS) signal a tight labor market, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days through elevated consumer spending and corporate profit margins.
failure_condition: SPX returns are negative when both UNRATE and PAYEMS show contrarian movements over 20-day horizons.

mechanism_narrative: Strong housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) indicate robust demand, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days through improved corporate revenue outlooks.
failure_condition: SPX returns are negative when both HOUST and RSAFS show positive momentum over 20-day horizons.

mechanism_narrative: High inflation (CPIAUCSL > 240) leads to immediate market sell-offs, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as investors adjust portfolios for inflation risk.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 240.

mechanism_narrative: Strong GDP growth (GDPC1) signals a robust economic recovery, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days through sustained corporate earnings and investor confidence.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns despite GDPC1 growth exceeding 2% quarter-over-quarter.
